rules.name: Terraform comment syntax
rules.descriptionSummary: Enforce usage of `#` for comments. The Terraform language supports `#`, `//`, and `/* */`; `#` is considered idiomatic for both single and multi-line comments.
rules.compliantExample: # Use # for single and multi-line comments

rules.name: Deprecated dot index syntax
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow legacy dot index syntax for list access. Use square brackets (e.g. `list[0]`, `list[*].a`) instead of `list.0` or `list.*.a`; dot syntax is no longer documented.
rules.nonCompliantExample:
output "x" { value = var.list.0 }
output "y" { value = var.items.*.id }
rules.compliantExample:
output "x" { value = var.list[0] }
output "y" { value = [for i in var.items : i.id] }

rules.name: Deprecated interpolation
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow deprecated 0.11-style interpolation. Interpolation-only expressions like `"${var.x}"` are redundant in Terraform 0.12+; use `var.x` instead.

rules.name: Deprecated lookup usage
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow `lookup(map, key)` without a default. Use native index syntax `map[key]` instead; `lookup` with two arguments has been deprecated since Terraform v0.7.
rules.compliantExample: locals { x = var.map["key"] }

rules.name: Outputs should have description
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow `output` declarations without description. Adding a description helps documentation and tools like terraform-docs.

rules.name: Variables should have description
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow `variable` declarations without description. Descriptions clarify purpose and are useful when combined with terraform-docs.

rules.name: Empty list equality check
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow comparisons with `[]` when checking if a collection is empty. Use `length(var.list) == 0` instead; `== []` has type semantics that often make it always false.
rules.compliantExample: locals { empty = length(var.list) == 0 }

rules.name: Terraform JSON syntax
rules.descriptionSummary: Enforce the official Terraform JSON syntax with a root object (keys: resource, variable, output, etc.). Array-at-root syntax is not the documented standard.
rules.compliantExample: {"resource": {"aws_instance": {"a": {}}}}

rules.name: Duplicate map keys
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow duplicate keys in a map. Duplicate keys are overwritten by Terraform and usually indicate a mistake.

rules.name: Module source should be pinned
rules.descriptionSummary: Module source attributes should be pinned to a specific version (e.g. ref=tag or commit SHA) rather than a floating branch. Unpinned sources can cause unexpected changes when the remote is updated and make builds non-reproducible.
rules.compliantExample: module "m" { source = "git::https://github.com/org/repo.git?ref=v1.0.0" }

rules.name: Module shallow clone
rules.descriptionSummary: Require pinned Git-hosted modules to use shallow cloning. Add `depth=1` to the source URL to reduce download size and improve CI performance.
rules.compliantExample:
module "m" { source = "git::https://github.com/org/repo.git?ref=v1.0.0//subdir" }
# Add depth=1 to the Git URL when using a ref

rules.name: Module version should be specified
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure modules from the Terraform Registry specify a `version`. Unpinned modules can pull breaking changes; constrain to a major version or exact version as needed.

rules.name: Terraform naming convention
rules.descriptionSummary: Enforces a naming convention for Terraform identifiers (resources, variables, outputs, locals, modules, data sources). Default format is snake_case; can be configured per block type. Non-compliant names (e.g. camelCase) are reported so that style stays consistent and readable.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_instance" "my_instance" {}

rules.name: Required providers source and version
rules.descriptionSummary: Require that all providers specify `source` and version constraint in `required_providers`. Avoid legacy provider block version and use the terraform block for constraints.

rules.name: Terraform required_version should be specified
rules.descriptionSummary: The terraform block should specify required_version (and optionally required_providers) so that the correct Terraform version is used. Without it, different users or CI may use different versions and get inconsistent behaviour or failures.

rules.name: Standard module structure
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure a module complies with the Terraform Standard Module Structure: main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf; variables and outputs in their corresponding files.
rules.nonCompliantExample: # All in one file or outputs in main.tf
rules.compliantExample: # Use main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf with variables and outputs in their files

rules.name: Variables should have type
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow `variable` declarations without type. Explicit types improve clarity and catch errors early.
rules.compliantExample: variable "name" { type = string; default = "x" }

rules.name: Unused declarations
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow variables, data sources, locals, and provider aliases that are declared but never used. Remove them or add an ignore annotation if intentional.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_instance" "a" { ami = "ami-123" }

rules.name: Unused required providers
rules.descriptionSummary: Check that all `required_providers` are used in the module. Remove providers that are no longer used to avoid unnecessary downloads.
rules.nonCompliantExample:
terraform { required_providers { aws = {}; null = {} } }
# null never used
rules.compliantExample: terraform { required_providers { aws = { source = "hashicorp/aws" } } }

rules.name: terraform.workspace with remote backend
rules.descriptionSummary: `terraform.workspace` should not be used with a remote backend when remote execution is enabled; Terraform Cloud uses the default workspace during remote runs. Use a variable instead.
rules.compliantExample:
variable "env" {}
resource "aws_instance" "a" { tags = { env = var.env } }

rules.name: ACM certificate lifecycle
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow invalid or deprecated ACM certificate lifecycle configuration.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_acm_certificate" "a" { lifecycle { create_before_destroy = true } }

rules.name: DB instance default parameter group
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow use of default parameter group for RDS DB instances; use a custom parameter group for control.
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_db_parameter_group" "a" { family = "postgres14" }
resource "aws_db_instance" "a" { engine = "postgres"; instance_class = "db.t3.micro"; parameter_group_name = aws_db_parameter_group.a.name }

rules.name: Invalid RDS DB engine
rules.descriptionSummary: The engine attribute of aws_db_instance must be a valid RDS engine (e.g. mysql, postgres). Invalid values cause apply failures.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_db_instance" "a" { engine = "postgres"; instance_class = "db.t3.micro" }

rules.name: Invalid RDS DB instance type
rules.descriptionSummary: The instance_class of aws_db_instance must be a valid RDS instance type. Invalid or previous-generation types cause apply failures.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_db_instance" "a" { engine = "postgres"; instance_class = "db.t3.micro" }

rules.name: Previous generation RDS instance type
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow previous-generation RDS instance classes; use current generation for performance and support.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_db_instance" "a" { engine = "postgres"; instance_class = "db.t3.micro" }

rules.name: Invalid Elastic Beanstalk environment name
rules.descriptionSummary: Elastic Beanstalk environment names must match the required format (length and character rules).
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_elastic_beanstalk_environment" "a" { name = "my-env-name" }

rules.name: ElastiCache cluster default parameter group
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow default parameter group for ElastiCache clusters; use a custom parameter group.
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_elasticache_parameter_group" "a" { family = "redis7" }
resource "aws_elasticache_cluster" "a" { cluster_id = "x"; node_type = "cache.t3.micro"; parameter_group_name = aws_elasticache_parameter_group.a.name }

rules.name: Invalid ElastiCache cluster node type
rules.descriptionSummary: The node_type of aws_elasticache_cluster must be a valid ElastiCache node type.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_elasticache_cluster" "a" { cluster_id = "x"; node_type = "cache.t3.micro" }

rules.name: Previous generation ElastiCache node type
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow previous-generation ElastiCache node types.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_elasticache_cluster" "a" { cluster_id = "x"; node_type = "cache.t3.micro" }

rules.name: ElastiCache replication group default parameter group
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow default parameter group for ElastiCache replication groups.
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_elasticache_parameter_group" "a" { family = "redis7" }
resource "aws_elasticache_replication_group" "a" { replication_group_id = "x"; node_type = "cache.t3.micro"; parameter_group_name = aws_elasticache_parameter_group.a.name }

rules.name: Invalid ElastiCache replication group node type
rules.descriptionSummary: The node_type must be a valid ElastiCache replication group node type.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_elasticache_replication_group" "a" { replication_group_id = "x"; node_type = "cache.t3.micro" }

rules.name: Previous generation ElastiCache replication group type
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow previous-generation node types for replication groups.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_elasticache_replication_group" "a" { replication_group_id = "x"; node_type = "cache.t3.micro" }

rules.name: Ephemeral resources
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow configuration that uses ephemeral storage or instance store in a way that can cause data loss.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_instance" "a" { ami = "ami-x"; root_block_device { volume_size = 20 } }

rules.name: IAM group policy too long
rules.descriptionSummary: Inline IAM group policies have size limits; policy document should not exceed the allowed size.
rules.nonCompliantExample: resource "aws_iam_group_policy" "a" { group = "x"; policy = jsonencode({ Version = "2012-10-17", Statement = [for i in range(100) : { Effect = "Allow", Action = "*", Resource = "*" }] }) }
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_iam_policy" "a" { policy = jsonencode({ Version = "2012-10-17", Statement = [{ Effect = "Allow", Action = "s3:GetObject", Resource = "*" }] }) }
resource "aws_iam_group_policy_attachment" "a" { group = "x"; policy_arn = aws_iam_policy.a.arn }

rules.name: IAM policy attachment exclusive
rules.descriptionSummary: Avoid using aws_iam_policy_attachment for multiple groups/roles/users; create separate attachments to prevent exclusive replacement.
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_iam_group_policy_attachment" "g1" { group = "g1"; policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnly" }
resource "aws_iam_group_policy_attachment" "g2" { group = "g2"; policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnly" }

rules.name: IAM policy document GovCloud ARNs
rules.descriptionSummary: IAM policy documents should use GovCloud-friendly ARN formats when targeting AWS GovCloud.
rules.compliantExample: data "aws_iam_policy_document" "a" { statement { actions = ["s3:*"]; resources = ["arn:aws-us-gov:s3:::*"] } }

rules.name: IAM policy GovCloud-friendly ARNs
rules.descriptionSummary: IAM policies should use GovCloud-friendly ARN formats where applicable.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_iam_policy" "a" { policy = jsonencode({ Statement = [{ Resource = "arn:aws-us-gov:s3:::*" }] }) }

rules.name: IAM policy SID invalid characters
rules.descriptionSummary: Policy statement IDs (Sid) must contain only alphanumeric and underscore characters.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_iam_policy" "a" { policy = jsonencode({ Statement = [{ Sid = "AllowAll" }] }) }

rules.name: IAM policy too long
rules.descriptionSummary: Inline IAM policy document exceeds size limits; consider using managed policies or splitting.
rules.nonCompliantExample: resource "aws_iam_role_policy" "a" { role = "x"; policy = jsonencode({ Version = "2012-10-17", Statement = [for i in range(50) : { Effect = "Allow", Action = "*", Resource = "*" }] }) }
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_iam_policy" "a" { policy = jsonencode({ Version = "2012-10-17", Statement = [{ Effect = "Allow", Action = "s3:GetObject", Resource = "*" }] }) }
resource "aws_iam_role_policy_attachment" "a" { role = "x"; policy_arn = aws_iam_policy.a.arn }

rules.name: IAM role deprecated policy attributes
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow deprecated attributes on IAM role or inline role policies.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_iam_role" "a" { assume_role_policy = jsonencode({ Version = "2012-10-17", Statement = [] }); name = "x" }

rules.name: IAM role policy GovCloud-friendly ARNs
rules.descriptionSummary: IAM role inline policies should use GovCloud-friendly ARN formats when applicable.
rules.nonCompliantExample: resource "aws_iam_role_policy" "a" { role = "x"; policy = jsonencode({ Statement = [{ Resource = "arn:aws:s3:::*" }] }) }
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_iam_role_policy" "a" { role = "x"; policy = jsonencode({ Statement = [{ Resource = "arn:aws-us-gov:s3:::*" }] }) }

rules.name: Invalid AWS instance type
rules.descriptionSummary: The instance_type attribute of aws_instance must be a valid AWS EC2 instance type. Invalid or deprecated types cause apply failures or runtime issues. See the AWS documentation for current instance types.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_instance" "a" { ami = "ami-123"; instance_type = "t3.micro" }

rules.name: Previous generation EC2 instance type
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow previous-generation EC2 instance types (e.g. t1, m1); use current generation for support and performance.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_instance" "a" { ami = "ami-123"; instance_type = "t3.micro" }

rules.name: Lambda deprecated runtime
rules.descriptionSummary: The runtime attribute of aws_lambda_function must not be a deprecated runtime (e.g. nodejs10.x); use a supported runtime.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_lambda_function" "a" { function_name = "x"; handler = "index.handler"; runtime = "nodejs20.x"; role = "arn:aws:iam::123:role/x" }

rules.name: AWS provider default tags
rules.descriptionSummary: Require default_tags on the AWS provider so that all supported resources receive consistent tags.
rules.compliantExample: provider "aws" { region = "us-east-1"; default_tags { tags = { Project = "myproject" } } }

rules.name: AWS resource missing tags
rules.descriptionSummary: Require specific tags for all AWS resource types that support them. Standardized tags help with cost and operations.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_instance" "a" { ami = "ami-123"; instance_type = "t3.micro"; tags = { Name = "my-instance", Env = "prod" } }

rules.name: Route target not specified
rules.descriptionSummary: aws_route must specify a target (e.g. gateway_id, nat_gateway_id); missing target is invalid.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_route" "a" { route_table_id = "rtb-123"; destination_cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"; gateway_id = "igw-123" }

rules.name: Route specified multiple targets
rules.descriptionSummary: aws_route should not specify more than one target type; only one of gateway_id, nat_gateway_id, etc.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_route" "a" { route_table_id = "rtb-123"; destination_cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"; gateway_id = "igw-123" }

rules.name: S3 bucket naming rules
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure S3 bucket names match naming rules (length 3–63, regex/prefix if configured). Bucket names must be globally unique.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_s3_bucket" "a" { bucket = "my-unique-bucket-name-123" }

rules.name: Security group inline rules
rules.descriptionSummary: Prefer separate aws_security_group_rule resources over inline ingress/egress blocks for better management.
rules.nonCompliantExample: resource "aws_security_group" "a" { name = "x"; ingress { from_port = 22; to_port = 22; protocol = "tcp"; cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"] } }
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_security_group" "a" { name = "x" }
resource "aws_security_group_rule" "a" { type = "ingress"; from_port = 22; to_port = 22; protocol = "tcp"; cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]; security_group_id = aws_security_group.a.id }

rules.name: Security group invalid protocol
rules.descriptionSummary: Security group rules must use valid protocol names or numbers (tcp, udp, icmp, etc.).
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_security_group_rule" "a" { type = "ingress"; from_port = 22; to_port = 22; protocol = "tcp"; security_group_id = "sg-123" }

rules.name: Security group rule deprecated
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow deprecated attribute usage in aws_security_group_rule.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_security_group_rule" "a" { type = "ingress"; from_port = 22; to_port = 22; protocol = "tcp"; cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]; security_group_id = "sg-123" }

rules.name: Security group rule invalid protocol
rules.descriptionSummary: Protocol must be valid for the security group rule.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_security_group_rule" "a" { type = "ingress"; from_port = 0; to_port = 0; protocol = "icmp"; security_group_id = "sg-123" }

rules.name: Write-only arguments
rules.descriptionSummary: Disallow arguments that are write-only (not readable after apply); can cause drift and state issues.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_instance" "a" { ami = "ami-123"; instance_type = "t3.micro" }

rules.name: Enforce HTTP Token IMDS
rules.descriptionSummary: aws_instance should activate session tokens for Instance Metadata Service (IMDS v2). Set metadata_options.http_tokens to "required" to improve security when talking to IMDS; default is optional.

rules.name: S3 Access block should block public ACL
rules.descriptionSummary: S3 buckets should have a public access block that blocks public ACLs. Use aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block with block_public_acls set to true so that public ACLs do not grant unintended access.
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "a" { bucket = "my-bucket" }
resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "a" { bucket = aws_s3_bucket.a.id; block_public_acls = true; block_public_policy = true; ignore_public_acls = true; restrict_public_buckets = true }

rules.name: S3 Access block should block public policy
rules.descriptionSummary: S3 buckets should have a public access block that blocks public bucket policies. Use aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block with block_public_policy set to true.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "a" { bucket = aws_s3_bucket.a.id; block_public_policy = true }

rules.name: S3 buckets should have encryption enabled
rules.descriptionSummary: S3 buckets should have server-side encryption enabled. Use aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration to enable SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS.
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "a" { bucket = "my-bucket" }
resource "aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration" "a" { bucket = aws_s3_bucket.a.id; rule { apply_server_side_encryption_by_default { sse_algorithm = "AES256" } } }

rules.name: S3 buckets should have logging enabled
rules.descriptionSummary: S3 buckets should have server access logging enabled. Add a logging block to the bucket resource pointing to a target bucket for log delivery.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_s3_bucket" "a" { bucket = "my-bucket"; logging { target_bucket = aws_s3_bucket.logs.id; target_prefix = "log/" } }

rules.name: Enable S3 bucket versioning
rules.descriptionSummary: S3 data should be versioned. Enable versioning on the bucket (versioning block or aws_s3_bucket_versioning) to preserve, retrieve, and restore object versions and recover from unintended changes.
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "a" { bucket = "my-bucket" }
resource "aws_s3_bucket_versioning" "a" { bucket = aws_s3_bucket.a.id; versioning_configuration { status = "Enabled" } }

rules.name: S3 Access block should ignore public ACLs
rules.descriptionSummary: S3 buckets should ignore public ACLs on buckets and objects. Use aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block with ignore_public_acls = true so that PUT calls with public ACLs are applied but the ACL is ignored.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "a" { bucket = aws_s3_bucket.a.id; block_public_acls = true; ignore_public_acls = true }

rules.name: No public access with ACL
rules.descriptionSummary: S3 buckets should not be publicly accessible through ACL. Use private ACL (acl = "private" or aws_s3_bucket_acl with acl = "private"); avoid canned public ACLs.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_s3_bucket_acl" "a" { bucket = aws_s3_bucket.a.id; acl = "private" }

rules.name: Restrict public buckets
rules.descriptionSummary: S3 Access block should restrict public bucket to limit access. Set restrict_public_buckets = true on aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block so that only the bucket owner and AWS services can access when a public policy exists.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "a" { bucket = aws_s3_bucket.a.id; restrict_public_buckets = true }

rules.name: Enable at-rest encryption for EC2
rules.descriptionSummary: Instance block devices should be encrypted. Set root_block_device and ebs_block_device encrypted = true on aws_instance so sensitive data is held securely at rest.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_instance" "a" { ami = "ami-123"; instance_type = "t3.micro"; root_block_device { encrypted = true }; ebs_block_device { device_name = "/dev/sdb"; encrypted = true } }

rules.name: EC2 instance metadata service configuration
rules.descriptionSummary: EC2 instances should use secure metadata service configuration (e.g. IMDSv2, hop limit) to reduce risk of metadata disclosure.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_instance" "a" { ami = "ami-123"; instance_type = "t3.micro"; metadata_options { http_tokens = "required"; http_put_response_hop_limit = 1 } }

rules.name: VPC flow logging
rules.descriptionSummary: VPCs should have flow logging enabled for network traffic monitoring and compliance. Create aws_flow_log resources for the VPC.
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_vpc" "a" { cidr_block = "10.0.0.0/16" }
resource "aws_flow_log" "a" { vpc_id = aws_vpc.a.id; traffic_type = "ALL"; log_destination_type = "cloud-watch-logs"; log_destination = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.a.arn }

rules.name: Ensure IAM policies that allow full "*-*" administrative privileges are not created
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure IAM policies that allow full "*-*" administrative privileges are not created.
rules.compliantExample: data "aws_iam_policy_document" "a" { statement { actions = ["s3:GetObject"]; resources = ["arn:aws:s3:::bucket/*"] } }

rules.name: Ensure ALB protocol is HTTPS
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure ALB protocol is HTTPS.
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "a" { bucket = "my-bucket" }
# Add the required configuration per Checkov policy (e.g. encryption, logging, versioning).

rules.name: Ensure all data stored in the EBS is securely encrypted
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure all data stored in the EBS is securely encrypted.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_ebs_volume" "a" { size = 10; availability_zone = "us-east-1a"; encrypted = true }

rules.name: Ensure all data stored in the Elasticsearch is securely encrypted at rest
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure all data stored in the Elasticsearch is securely encrypted at rest.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_ebs_volume" "a" { size = 10; availability_zone = "us-east-1a"; encrypted = true }

rules.name: Ensure all Elasticsearch has node-to-node encryption enabled
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure all Elasticsearch has node-to-node encryption enabled.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_ebs_volume" "a" { size = 10; availability_zone = "us-east-1a"; encrypted = true }

rules.name: Ensure rotation for customer created CMKs is enabled
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure rotation for customer created CMKs is enabled.
rules.compliantExample:
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "a" { bucket = "my-bucket" }
# Add the required configuration per Checkov policy (e.g. encryption, logging, versioning).

rules.name: Ensure all data stored in the Launch configuration or instance Elastic Blocks Store is securely encrypted
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure all data stored in the Launch configuration or instance Elastic Blocks Store is securely encrypted.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_ebs_volume" "a" { size = 10; availability_zone = "us-east-1a"; encrypted = true }

rules.name: Ensure IAM password policy expires passwords within 90 days or less
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure IAM password policy expires passwords within 90 days or less.
rules.compliantExample: data "aws_iam_policy_document" "a" { statement { actions = ["s3:GetObject"]; resources = ["arn:aws:s3:::bucket/*"] } }

rules.name: Ensure IAM password policy requires minimum length of 14 or greater
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure IAM password policy requires minimum length of 14 or greater.
rules.compliantExample: data "aws_iam_policy_document" "a" { statement { actions = ["s3:GetObject"]; resources = ["arn:aws:s3:::bucket/*"] } }

rules.name: Ensure IAM password policy requires at least one lowercase letter
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure IAM password policy requires at least one lowercase letter.
rules.compliantExample: data "aws_iam_policy_document" "a" { statement { actions = ["s3:GetObject"]; resources = ["arn:aws:s3:::bucket/*"] } }

rules.name: Ensure IAM password policy requires at least one number
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure IAM password policy requires at least one number.
rules.compliantExample: data "aws_iam_policy_document" "a" { statement { actions = ["s3:GetObject"]; resources = ["arn:aws:s3:::bucket/*"] } }

rules.name: Ensure IAM password policy prevents password reuse
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure IAM password policy prevents password reuse.
rules.compliantExample: data "aws_iam_policy_document" "a" { statement { actions = ["s3:GetObject"]; resources = ["arn:aws:s3:::bucket/*"] } }

rules.name: Ensure IAM password policy requires at least one symbol
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure IAM password policy requires at least one symbol.
rules.compliantExample: data "aws_iam_policy_document" "a" { statement { actions = ["s3:GetObject"]; resources = ["arn:aws:s3:::bucket/*"] } }

rules.name: Ensure IAM password policy requires at least one uppercase letter
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure IAM password policy requires at least one uppercase letter.
rules.compliantExample: data "aws_iam_policy_document" "a" { statement { actions = ["s3:GetObject"]; resources = ["arn:aws:s3:::bucket/*"] } }

rules.name: Ensure all data stored in the RDS is securely encrypted at rest
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure all data stored in the RDS is securely encrypted at rest.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_ebs_volume" "a" { size = 10; availability_zone = "us-east-1a"; encrypted = true }

rules.name: Ensure all data stored in RDS is not publicly accessible
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure all data stored in RDS is not publicly accessible.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_s3_bucket_acl" "a" { bucket = aws_s3_bucket.a.id; acl = "private" }

rules.name: Ensure the S3 bucket has access logging enabled
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure the S3 bucket has access logging enabled.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_s3_bucket" "a" { bucket = "my-bucket"; logging { target_bucket = aws_s3_bucket.logs.id; target_prefix = "log/" } }

rules.name: Ensure all data stored in the S3 bucket is securely encrypted at rest
rules.descriptionSummary: Ensure all data stored in the S3 bucket is securely encrypted at rest.
rules.compliantExample: resource "aws_ebs_volume" "a" { size = 10; availability_zone = "us-east-1a"; encrypted = true }
